enOverride: used before a singular noun to refer to a single, non-specific thing

ex.en: I saw a dog in the park.
ex.ko: 나는 공원에서 개 한 마리를 봤다.

ex.en: This book is about space.
The plane flew above the clouds.
We must take action now.
Swimming is a fun activity.
He is a famous actor.
Add some salt to the soup.
What is your home address?
She is afraid of spiders.
We ate dinner after the movie.
ex.ko: 우리는 영화가 끝난 후에 저녁을 먹었다.

ex.en: He spent a quiet afternoon in the park.
Can you say that again?
What is your age?
I met her three years ago.
I agree with you.
The flight was delayed due to trouble with the airplane.
We arrived at the airport early.
She released a new album.
All the students passed the test.
Are you all right?
The job is almost done.
She lives alone.
We walked along the river.
She has already graduated.
He has a car, and also a bike.
She always arrives on time.

enOverride: the first-person singular present form of the verb 'be'

ex.en: I am a student.

enOverride: the form of 'a' used before a word starting with a vowel sound

ex.en: She ate an apple.

enOverride: used to connect two words, phrases, or clauses

ex.en: I like tea and coffee.
He was angry at the weather.
A dog is a kind of animal.
Can I have another cup of coffee?
ex.ko: 커피 한 잔 더 마실 수 있을까요?

ex.en: I waited several days for his answer.
ex.ko: 나는 그의 대답을 며칠 동안 기다렸다.

ex.en: Do you have any questions?
Is anybody home?
Anyone can join the club.
ex.ko: 누구나 그 동아리에 가입할 수 있다.

enOverride: any thing at all, no matter what

ex.en: Do you need anything?
She ate a red apple.
Her birthday is in April.
She wore an apron while cooking.

enOverride: the present form of 'be' used with 'you', 'we', or 'they'

ex.en: You are my best friend.
He broke his arm.
There were a few people standing around.
We arrived at the station on time.
She studies art at university.
I read an interesting article today.
She works as a teacher.
She asked him for a loan.

enOverride: used to show an exact place or time

ex.en: Let's meet at the cafe.
School starts in August.
My aunt visited us last week.
The leaves fall in autumn.
He lay awake thinking about his new job.
ex.ko: 그는 새 직장을 생각하며 깨어 누워 있었다.

ex.en: The dog ran away from the lion.
The baby began to cry again.
She went back to her parents' house.
He got a bad report card.
He stuffed his laundry into a large bag.
ex.ko: 그는 큰 가방에 빨래를 쑤셔 넣었다.

ex.en: The ball rolled across the floor.
She eats a banana every morning.
He plays guitar in a band.
I need to go to the bank.
They met at a bar downtown.
Teams take turns at bat trying to score runs.
ex.ko: 팀들은 번갈아 타석에 서서 점수를 내려 한다.

ex.en: Points are scored by throwing the ball through a hoop.
ex.ko: 공을 링 안으로 던져서 점수를 얻는다.

ex.en: A bat flew out of the cave.
ex.ko: 박쥐 한 마리가 동굴에서 날아 나왔다.

ex.en: She took a warm bath after work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 퇴근 후 따뜻한 목욕을 했다.

ex.en: The bathroom is down the hall.

enOverride: used to describe what something or someone is, or that it exists

ex.en: I want to be a doctor.
We spent the day at the beach.
She cooked some black beans.
A bear crossed the trail.
She has a beautiful voice.

enOverride: used to give the reason for something

ex.en: I stayed home because I was sick.
He became annoyed when he heard the bad news.
ex.ko: 그는 나쁜 소식을 듣고 짜증이 났다.

ex.en: He sat on the edge of the bed.
My bedroom is upstairs.
A bee landed on the flower.
We had beef for dinner.
I had known her before.
We began working at dawn.
The dog hid behind the sofa.
I believe you.
The bell rang loudly.
The fish swam below the surface.
She sat beside her brother.
This is the best film of the year.
She is better at math than me.
The cat sat between the two chairs.
He rides his bicycle to school.
He moved to the big city.
She rode her bike to the park.
A bird sang outside my window.
They divorced after the birth of the child.
Happy birthday to you!
She had tea and biscuits.
She wore a black dress.
Fill in the blank spaces.
The pyramids were built with large stone blocks.
Blow on the soup to cool it down.
The sky is blue today.
The board has seven members.
We took a boat across the lake.
He felt as if his whole body were on fire.
ex.ko: 그는 온몸이 불타는 것 같은 느낌이었다.

ex.en: The dog buried a bone in the yard.
I am reading a good book on economics.
ex.ko: 나는 경제학에 관한 좋은 책을 읽고 있다.

ex.en: She bought a novel at the bookstore.
It was a boring evening with uninteresting people.
May I borrow your lawn mower?
ex.ko: 당신의 잔디 깎는 기계를 빌려도 될까요?

ex.en: Both girls are pretty.
She filled the bottle with water.
The keys are at the bottom of the bag.
She poured cereal into a bowl.
He put the toys in a box.
The baby was a boy.
She introduced her boyfriend to her parents.
The brain controls the whole body.
She baked fresh bread this morning.
ex.ko: 그녀는 오늘 아침 신선한 빵을 구웠다.

ex.en: Be careful not to break the glass.
He always eats breakfast at seven.
I can breathe better when the air is clean.
We crossed the bridge over the river.
The sun was bright and hot.
Bring me the box from the other room.
His arm is broken.
My brother still lives with our parents.
ex.ko: 내 남동생은 아직 부모님과 함께 산다.

ex.en: She has brown eyes.
He brushed the hairs from his jacket.
She filled the bucket with water.
This little pig built his house out of straw.
ex.ko: 이 아기 돼지는 짚으로 집을 지었다.
